🚨 Upozorňujeme na zranitelnosti v NetScaler ADC a NetScaler Gateway, CVE‑2026‑3055 a CVE‑2026‑4368.  CVE‑2026‑3055: zranitelnost vychází z nedostatečné validace vstupů, která může vést k memory overread stavu. Útočník ji může zneužít vzdáleně bez autentizace k získání citlivých informací nebo k narušení stability systému, což může mít dopad na důvěrnost i dostupnost. Aby bylo zneužití úspěšné, musí být zařízení NetScaler ADC nebo NetScaler Gateway nakonfigurováno jako poskytovatel identit SAML (SAML IDP). Postiženy jsou verze NetScaler ADC a NetScaler Gateway 14.1 před 14.1‑66.59, 13.1 před 13.1‑62.23 a FIPS/NDcPP před 13.1‑37.262.  CVE‑2026‑4368: zranitelnost je způsobena race condition chybou, která může vést k záměně uživatelských relací. Útočník tak může za určitých provozních podmínek získat neoprávněný přístup k relaci jiného uživatele, což představuje zásadní riziko pro integritu i důvěrnost. Aby bylo zneužití úspěšné, musí být zařízení NetScaler ADC nebo NetScaler Gateway nakonfigurováno jako gateway (SSL VPN, ICA Proxy, CVPN, a RDP Proxy) nebo AAA server. Zasaženy jsou systémy NetScaler ADC a NetScaler Gateway ve verzi 14.1‑66.54.  📌Doporučujeme aktualizovat na verze 14.1‑66.59, 13.1‑62.23 nebo 13.1‑37.262 a novější.

Best approach: AI/ML-driven systems for real-time behavioral analysis, anomaly detection, and autonomous mitigation—no human delays.  Use global edge providers like Cloudflare or Akamai for always-on L3-7 scrubbing at the nearest PoP. Layer with adaptive rate limiting, deep packet inspection, and traffic baselining. Build redundancy and test your incident plan regularly.  Static defenses won't cut it; evolve your protections too.

선거 사이버 보안 전문가: 도미니언 투표 시스템에 결과 조작을 가능하게 하는 백도어가 숨겨져 있다  선거 사이버 보안 전문가 마크 쿡(@PatriotMarkCook)은 도미니언 투표 시스템에 시작 메뉴에서 바로 접근 가능한 백도어 소프트웨어가 포함되어 있어, 단 몇 번의 클릭만으로 다음과 같은 작업을 수행할 수 있다고 경고했습니다.  • 후보자 이름 변경 • 투표 용지 정의 변경 • 투표소 곳곳의 USB 드라이브에서 암호화 키 추출 • 선거 결과 전면 뒤집기  “이것은 우연이 아닙니다. 선거를 조작하기 쉽게 만든 시스템을 만든 사람들이 그렇게 멍청할 리가 없습니다.”  쿡은 또한 투표 용지 주문형 프린터에 대해서도 우려를 표명했습니다. 그는 2020년 12월 새크라멘토 카운티 선거관리국의 보안 구역에서 투표 용지가 가득 담긴 프린터를 직접 목격했으며, 컴퓨터 비밀번호가 키보드 위에 테이프로 붙어 있는 것을 보았다고 밝혔습니다.   그는 이 시스템이 "거짓의 피라미드" 위에 세워져 있다고 말합니다. 공급업체, 시험 기관, 인증 기관, 그리고 선거 관계자들이 모두 실제 보안 검증 없이 승인만 한다는 것입니다.  쿡은 현재 각 주를 순회하며 의원들에게 직접 증거를 제시하고 있으며, 마침내 진실을 알아야 할 사람들을 직접 만나고 있다고 말합니다.  "이 모든 것은 농담이고, 그 농담의 대상은 미국 국민입니다. 저는 웃을 수 없습니다."

TeamPCP threat actor expands supply chain attack from Trivy to Checkmarx GitHub Actions, using stolen credentials to cascade compromise across CI/CD pipelines. Second wave uses identical stealer payload with vendor-specific typosquat domains.  Key technical details: • Initial Trivy compromise (March 19) harvested credentials via /proc/*/mem scraping, IMDS queries to 169.254.169.254, and webhook enumeration • Checkmarx wave (March 23) used stolen PATs to poison ast-github-action v2.3.28 with identical tpcp.tar.gz payload • Exfiltration to typosquats: scan.aquasecurtiy[.]org (45.148.10.212) and checkmarx[.]zone using AES-256+RSA-4096 encryption • Same kill chain: Runner.Worker → bash → setup script → curl POST with identical headers and binary upload pattern  Attack methodology: • Force-pushed 75/76 version tags in compromised repositories to redirect to malicious commits • Memory extraction targets GitHub PATs, AWS/GCP credentials, and webhook URLs from runner processes • Cascading compromise: stolen credentials enable poisoning additional actions in accessible repos • Vendor-specific domains reduce detection likelihood in CI/CD logs  Hunt for curl POST requests from CI runners to external domains with application/octet-stream content-type and tpcp.tar.gz filenames in process arguments.  #DFIR_Radar

The event resulted in a fire that has since been brought under control by our on-site emergency response teams, working closely with local fire departments and authorities.  We are pleased to report that no injuries occurred to our employees, contractors, or members of the surrounding community. A precautionary shelter-in-place advisory was issued for nearby residents as a standard safety measure and has now been lifted.  The safety of our workforce and neighbors is Valero’s top priority. Our teams acted swiftly to isolate the affected equipment, contain the situation, and minimize any potential impact. Air monitoring conducted in and around the facility showed no elevated levels of concern, and operations in unaffected areas continue normally.  We are conducting a thorough investigation into the cause of the heater-related event, in coordination with regulatory agencies. Preliminary indications point to an equipment issue that is common in complex refining processes. We will share additional details as they become available and are committed to implementing any learnings to further strengthen our operations.  Valero has a long-standing record of safe and reliable operations at the Port Arthur refinery, which plays a vital role in supplying essential fuels to communities across the region. We appreciate the support and cooperation of local first responders, officials, and residents during this event. We remain dedicated to maintaining the highest standards of safety, environmental stewardship, and operational excellence.  For more information, please contact Valero Media Relations.

Authorities have moved quickly to secure the area and implement precautionary measures for nearby communities.  Local officials have advised residents to remain indoors as safety checks and assessments continue. Traffic restrictions have also been introduced to prevent further risk around the affected zone. Whoa whoa whoa. Everyone CLAM down for a second.  Earlier today someone broke the news that there was a supply chain attack impacting LiteLLM which had over 97 MILLION installs. Initially it was reported the payload was vibe coded which resulted in the payload failing.  HOWEVER, this has been determined to be NOT TRUE. The payload was a SUCCESS. The payload failed in specific edge cases (currently unknown). The Threat Actor(s) managed to exfiltrate data from 500,000 infected machines (approx. 300gb of data).  I have confirmed this from three different sources. The initially news which is spreading all over social media is incorrect and this is actually a very big bamboozle.  They had one short, one opportunity, and did indeed seize it (but only failing in specific and unknown edge cases).  It's all over for LLM-dependency nerds. Also, in a bit of irony, LiteLLM is SOC2 certified by Delve.  This is very big shenanigans for a Tuesday.
